audit_metadata.independence: no reliance on first audit conclusions

warnings.detail: eps_dispersion missing 71.7% (885332/1234580 rows). This column is not in any factor group used by V0.4.1, so impact is nil.
fmp_covered missing 71.0%. Not in factor groups, no impact.

netProfitMargin_qoq, grossProfitMargin_qoq, operatingProfitMargin_qoq, ebitdaMargin_qoq missing 71.0%. These ARE in the qoq factor group. However, these are QoQ delta columns that are only available when quarterly earnings data exists. Missing = no prior quarter data.
vwap missing 56.7%. Not in factor groups, no impact.
i_revenue_growth_yoy coverage 84.2% (2025) and 84.8% (2026). This is the lowest-coverage factor in V0.4.1's active factor set (income group). Below 90% threshold but above 70% reject line.

checks.fundamental_2025_2026_coverage: 22/22 active factors at 93.7%-100%. Only i_revenue_growth_yoy at 84.2-84.8%
checks.outlier_check: r_priceToEarningsRatio: 0.2% >3sigma (normal for PE). g_revenueGrowth: 0.0% >3sigma. No extreme outliers.

warnings.detail: Analyst fill method: median_per_date. Each date filled with its own cross-sectional median. NO temporal leakage because the fill is purely cross-sectional (no future data used).
FLIP_FACTORS: 21/21 factors present in data. All inversion logic correct (higher = worse for PE/PB/PS/Debt/Inventory/CapEx/Dispersion).
growth_composite definition: 0.5*fund_growth + 0.3*analyst + 0.2*income. This is a linear combination of percentile ranks (0-1 scale). The weighting is subjective but internally consistent.
Cross-sectional ranks computed per date independently. No forward-looking information in rank computation.
Factor groups verified: all 8 groups (fund_ratio/fund_metric/fund_growth/analyst/balance/cashflow/income/qoq) have 100% column coverage in the parquet.

pit_check.result: PASS - each date's rank uses only that date's cross-sectional data

details: Analyst fill: cross-sectional median per date, no future data
Walk-Forward: train_end -> test_start, no overlap in train/test
Rank Inversion: forward returns are intentional (predictive test)
Combo factors (growth_composite): computed from group ranks, no future data

warnings.detail: WF Sharpe 1.603 with std 1.293. High std means window-level performance varies significantly. Sharpe range: -1.587 to 4.169. This is acceptable for a multi-year backtest but indicates regime-dependent performance.
MaxDD -31.07% is the worst among three models (V0.3.1: -29.08%, V0.4.0: -28.87%). V0.4.1 takes on more downside risk for higher Sharpe. Acceptable trade-off but worth noting.

Rank Inversion: 63.2% pass rate (12/19 windows). Threshold is >60%. This is a marginal pass. Window 8 (2020-07 to 2021-01) has the worst spread (-0.0547), likely due to COVID recovery momentum favoring beaten-down stocks.
Sharpe degradation: PASS (-13.5%). Recent windows (1.699) slightly outperform early windows (1.497). No degradation detected.
V0.4.1 vs V0.3.1: Sharpe 1.603 vs 1.465 (+9.4%), RI 63.2% vs 42.1% (+50%), CAGR 28.9% vs 26.7% (+8.2%). V0.4.1 is strictly better on all metrics except MaxDD.
V0.4.1 vs V0.4.0: Sharpe 1.603 vs 1.267 (+26.5%), RI 63.2% vs 47.4% (+33.3%). V0.4.1 is strictly better on all metrics except MaxDD.

rank_inversion.note: 63.2% is above the 60% threshold but only by 3.2 percentage points. This is a thin margin.

comparison.verdict: V0.4.1 is the only model that passes Rank Inversion. Higher Sharpe, higher CAGR, but also higher MaxDD.

warnings.detail: Scoring logic: V0.4.1 = 0.70*fund_ratio + 0.30*growth_composite. All weights sum to 1.00.
growth_composite = 0.5*fund_growth + 0.3*analyst + 0.2*income. All weights sum to 1.00.
Output format: final_validation_results.json contains metadata, models (3), comparison, best_model, best_score. All fields present and valid.
Best model selection: scoring formula = Sharpe*0.4 + (1+MaxDD)*0.2 + RI_pass_rate*0.2 + RI_bonus*0.2, with degradation penalty. V0.4.1 scored 1.105 (highest).
Backtest engine: cost=0.1%, stop_loss=-15%, top_n=10, hold_days=30. Standard parameters.
Data->Features: All 86 factor columns (20+19+15+4+4+4+6+4) present in parquet. 21 FLIP_FACTORS all present. 0 missing factors.

Features->Model: Walk-Forward windows are adjacent (not overlapping). Boundary dates (e.g., 2017-01-04) appear as the last day of one test window and first day of the next. This is standard practice and creates at most 1-day boundary overlap per window transition. Acceptable.
Model->Signal: Best model selection formula is documented and reproducible. V0.4.1 correctly selected as highest-scoring.
Pipeline integrity: features_v04_1.parquet -> final_validation.py -> final_validation_results.json. All files exist and are internally consistent.

RI window boundary overlap: 18 out of 19 window transitions share a boundary date. This means 1 date per transition is counted in both windows' RI test. Impact: minimal (1/180 test dates per window = 0.6% overlap). Not a data leak but technically not pure OOS.

details: Analyst fill: cross-sectional only, no temporal leakage
Rank computation: per-date independent, no forward look
Boundary overlap: 1-date boundary shared between adjacent windows, minimal impact (<1%)

summary.verdict_detail: V0.4.1 passes all 5 layers with 0 blockers. 5 warnings are all INFO-level or minor WARN-level. The Rank Inversion pass rate (63.2%) is above threshold (60%) but only marginally. MaxDD (-31.07%) is the highest among three models. All other metrics (Sharpe, CAGR, RI, degradation) are superior to V0.3.1 and V0.4.0.
summary.first_audit_comparison: First audit found 15 warnings, 0 blockers. This second audit found 5 warnings (reduced by consolidating INFO-level items) and 20 INFO-level items. Both audits agree: CONDITIONAL PASS. No new blockers discovered.
key_risks: Rank Inversion margin is thin (63.2% vs 60% threshold)
MaxDD -31.07% is highest among compared models
WF Sharpe std 1.293 indicates high regime-dependency
1-date boundary overlap in WF windows (standard practice, minimal impact)
